summary: Rough‑ish but lively first European gig featuring a breathtaking Dark Star, captured on an uneven recording.
review: The Hollywood Festival show is remembered as the Dead’s inaugural European outing – high energy, sunset‑to‑night vibes and a standout Dark Star that many fans still rave about. The tape quality varies; some listeners hear a crisp “aud” feel while others note roughness and occasional motor‑bike noise, suggesting an imperfect source.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance (≈4.5/5) while recording quality is mixed (≈3.2/5). Weighted combine yields ~4.1, rounded to 4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the sound as rough or middle‑of‑the‑road with occasional motorbike noise and a few technical glitches, but also note good instrument placement that brings the show back.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First European festival set that built momentum from sunset into night; high‑energy and memorable despite a few technical hiccups.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is highlighted – a breathtaking Dark Star and possibly his first recorded use of the Gibson SG after switching from his Strat.
